title: ageratum conyzoides subsp. latifolium
intro: Ageratum conyzoides subsp. latifolium is a subspecies of the Ageratum conyzoides plant, commonly known as goatweed or bitter leaf. This plant is used in traditional medicine and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Ageratum conyzoides subsp. latifolium include its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall health and well-being.
